svg.text: What is the telescope that looks at space called? When did the James Webb Telescope launch? What does the MIRROR collect from far away stars and galaxies?
THE JAMES WEBB SPACE TELESCOPE / JWST DECEMBER 25, 2021 / CHRISTMAS DAY LIGHT FROM BILLIONS OF YEARS AGO
Name something the text tells us the telescope can look at. Name two things the text says about the mirror. What keeps the telescope steady in space?
STARS / PLANETS / GALAXIES / DISTANT LIGHT MADE OF 18 PIECES / MADE OF GOLD AND BERYLLIUM GRAVITY FROM EARTH AND THE SUN
How many miles away from Earth is the Lagrange Point? How many pieces make up the James Webb Telescope mirror? How many planets are in our solar system?
ONE MILLION MILES 18 PIECES 8 PLANETS
Close your eyes. Imagine floating one million miles from Earth. What do you see around you? Imagine you are a leaf soaking up warm sunlight all day long. What does it feel like?
STARS / DARKNESS / EARTH FROM FAR AWAY / THE SUN / GALAXIES WARM / BRIGHT / ALIVE / ENERGIZED / PEACEFUL / HAPPY
If you could explore any part of space where would you go? What does freedom mean to you personally? What would you most want to say if you had a letterboard right now?
STUDENT CHOICE STUDENT CHOICE STUDENT CHOICE
What planet do humans live on? What country declared independence in 1776? What gas do plants release during photosynthesis? What is at the center of our solar system?
EARTH THE UNITED STATES OF AMERICA OXYGEN THE SUN
What process do plants use to make their own food? What tool is used in Spelling to Communicate? What does the topic sentence tell the reader in a paragraph?
PHOTOSYNTHESIS A LETTERBOARD WHAT THE PARAGRAPH WILL BE ABOUT
Name two things plants need for photosynthesis. Name two things LITERACY helps a person do. Name two emotions humans can feel every single day of their lives.
SUNLIGHT / WATER / CARBON DIOXIDE READ / WRITE / COMMUNICATE / LEARN JOY / SADNESS / ANGER / FEAR / HAPPINESS
How many original colonies became the United States? How many letters are in the English alphabet? How many basic emotions do most scientists agree humans have?
13 COLONIES 26 LETTERS 6 BASIC EMOTIONS
Think of a happy moment. What do you feel in your body? Imagine spelling your first full sentence on a letterboard. What do you feel inside your heart and mind?
WARMTH / JOY / SMILING / ENERGY / LIGHTNESS PROUD / EXCITED / HAPPY / RELIEVED / POWERFUL / CONFIDENT
What emotion do you feel most often and why? What does communication mean to you personally? If you were a plant where would you want to grow and why?
STUDENT CHOICE STUDENT CHOICE STUDENT CHOICE
What part of the body processes language? Which planet is the only one known to have life? What document told the world that Americans wanted to be free and self-governing?
THE BRAIN EARTH THE DECLARATION OF INDEPENDENCE
What are emotions? What does literacy mean? What is a paragraph? What does the Artemis Mission plan to do? What is the first sentence of a paragraph called?
FEELINGS WE EXPERIENCE EVERY DAY THE ABILITY TO READ WRITE AND COMMUNICATE A GROUP OF SENTENCES ABOUT ONE IDEA SEND ASTRONAUTS TO THE MOON
Name two things the text says Spelling to Communicate gives a person. Name two things the colonists wanted from the American Revolution. Name two facts about the Artemis Mission.
A VOICE / ABILITY TO SHARE INTELLIGENCE FREEDOM / INDEPENDENCE / RIGHTS / SELF-GOVERNMENT NASA RUNS IT / ASTRONAUTS WILL GO TO THE MOON
How many senses does the human body have? How many years ago did dinosaurs go extinct? How many bones are in the adult human body? How many colors are in a rainbow?
5 SENSES 66 MILLION YEARS AGO 206 BONES 7 COLORS
Imagine standing on Mars looking back at Earth. What do you see and feel? Close your eyes and imagine spelling your name for the very first time on a letterboard right now.
A TINY BLUE DOT / WONDER / AWE / LONELINESS / AMAZEMENT PROUD / EXCITED / SEEN / HEARD / POWERFUL / EMOTIONAL
If you could send one message to the world using a letterboard what would it say? What is your favorite topic to learn about and why would you want a lesson written about it?
STUDENT CHOICE STUDENT CHOICE
What is the job of the topic sentence? What keeps the James Webb Telescope steady at L2? What is the green part of a plant that captures sunlight called? What is gravity?
TO TELL THE READER WHAT THE PARAGRAPH IS ABOUT GRAVITY FROM EARTH AND THE SUN CHLOROPHYLL A FORCE THAT PULLS OBJECTS TOGETHER
What does a speller do on the letterboard? What is photosynthesis? What was the name of the army George Washington led? What is the Lagrange Point also called?
POINTS TO LETTERS TO BUILD WORDS AND SENTENCES HOW PLANTS MAKE FOOD THE CONTINENTAL ARMY L2
Name two ways people can communicate with each other. Name two things the Sun provides for living things. Name something the text says about the solar system.
TALKING / WRITING / SIGNING / SPELLING / TYPING LIGHT / HEAT HAS 8 PLANETS / SUN IS AT THE CENTER / EARTH IS THIRD FROM THE SUN
Imagine you are the first person to walk on the Moon. What do you see, hear, and feel all around you as you take your very first step on the lunar surface in your spacesuit?
